question: Explain this print.
candidate_response:
## 1. What this appears to be
This appears to be a wiring diagram for a 3-phase starter, specifically Bulletin 509 Sizes 7 and 8, as indicated at the bottom of the page.

## 2. Main visible components
The main visible components include:
- A 3-phase motor
- Start and stop pushbuttons
- A control transformer
- Overload protection (O.L.'s)
- Control relay (CR)
- Motor starter coils (K1, K2)
- Economizer capacitors (Econ. Cap.)
- Fuses (FU)

## 3. Plain-English theory of operation
The plain-English theory of operation appears to be as follows:
- When the start button is pressed, the control relay (CR) energizes, which in turn energizes the motor starter coils (K1, K2).
- The motor starter coils then close the main contacts to power the 3-phase motor.
- The overload protection (O.L.'s) is in series with the motor starter coils to protect the motor from overcurrent conditions.
- When the stop button is pressed or an overload condition occurs, the motor starter coils de-energize, and the motor stops.

## 4. What must be true for it to work
For the circuit to work, the following conditions must be met:
- The start button must be pressed
- The stop button must not be pressed
- The overload protection (O.L.'s) must not be tripped
- The control relay (CR) must be energized
- The motor starter coils (K1, K2) must be energized

## 5. What would stop it from working
The following conditions would stop the circuit from working:
- Open circuit in the control transformer
- Blown fuses (FU)
- Tripped overload protection (O.L.'s)
- Faulty motor starter coils (K1, K2)
- Faulty control relay (CR)

## 6. Unclear or unreadable items
Nothing major appears to be unreadable in the provided image.
